title: CTE with SELECT * prevents subcolumns pruning
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
When using a CTE with `SELECT *`, the entire JSON column is read instead of just the needed subcolumns even when the field has type hint on the JSON definition.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_json_pruning
(
id UInt64,
event JSON(
class_name String,
category_name String,
message String,
)
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
order by (id);
```
```
explain header=1
with foo as (
select * from test_json_pruning
)
select event.class_name
from foo
Expression ((Project names + (Projection + (Change column names to column identifiers + (Project names + (Projection + Change column names to column identifiers))))))
Header: event.class_name String
ReadFromPreparedSource (Read from NullSource)
Header: event JSON(category_name String, class_name String, message String)
```
```
explain header=1
select event.class_name
from test_json_pruning
Expression ((Project names + (Projection + Change column names to column identifiers)))
Header: event.class_name String
ReadFromPreparedSource (Read from NullSource)
Header: event.class_name String
```
### Expected behavior
_No response_

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_json_pruning_2 (id BIGINT, event JSON);
INSERT INTO test_json_pruning_2 (id, event) VALUES (1, '{"class_name": "error", "category_name": "system", "message": "An error occurred", "timestamp": "2023-10-01 12:00:00"}');
INSERT INTO test_json_pruning_2 (id, event) VALUES (2, '{"class_name": "info", "category_name": "user", "message": "User logged in", "timestamp": "2023-10-01 12:05:00"}');
WITH foo AS (SELECT * FROM test_json_pruning_2) SELECT event->>'class_name' AS class_name FROM foo;
WITH bar AS (SELECT id, event->>'category_name' AS category_name FROM test_json_pruning_2 WHERE id = 1) SELECT event->>'class_name' AS class_name FROM bar;
key_bug_features: CTE with SELECT * reads entire JSON column
